message:
fix(channel): replace timing-based sleep with deterministic sync in broadcast test

The test_sender_returns_closed_after_receiver_dropped test relied on a
10ms sleep to give the sender thread time to enter send() before
dropping the receiver. This created a race condition:
- On fast machines the sleep might be too long (wasting CI time)
- On loaded machines the sleep might be too short (flaky failure)

Replace with a synchronization channel: the sender signals
send_entered_tx immediately before calling permit.send(), and the main
thread waits on send_entered_rx before dropping the receiver. This
makes the test fully deterministic with zero timing dependency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
